page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the N2 machine family. It features 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.8 GHz, the instance utilizes x86_64 architecture with 8 physical cores and hyper-threading enabled with 2 threads per core. The CPU allocation is dedicated, and the processor includes a 33 MB L3 cache along with hardware support for AVX-512 vector extensions and AES cryptography. This instance does not include bundled local storage or GPUs.

Performance benchmarks place the n2-standard-16 consistently in the average tier across most workloads. It achieves a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 15060.78 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 7239.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 84.64 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads and 43.14 GB/sec for uncached writes. The instance demonstrates solid capabilities in static web serving at 12.34 GB/sec and handles Redis SET operations at 14.19 million operations per second. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression and medium-sized 7B LLM inference tasks show weaker performance, with the 7B model text generation reaching only 7.85 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n2-standard-16 offers a balanced resource profile suitable for mainstream enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory or compute-optimized hardware. Given its balanced CPU-to-RAM ratio and average multi-core performance, it is well-suited for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, database operations, software compilation, and moderate data processing tasks. The lack of local storage and GPU acceleration makes it less ideal for high-performance storage-heavy databases or deep learning training.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance configured with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio. It is powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor with 8 physical cores, 2 threads per core, and a 33 MB L3 cache. The instance features x86_64 architecture with AVX-512 and AES instruction support, but does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data indicates average performance across standard operations, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 7239.0, static web serving throughput of 12.34 GB/sec, and Redis SET performance of 14.19 million operations per second. While it exhibits weaker performance on single-threaded decompression and medium LLM inference, its balanced resource density makes it a cost-effective option for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-16 is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced performance for standard enterprise workloads.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring sixteen dedicated vCPUs and sixty-four gigabytes of memory for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs and 64 GB RAM providing a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Intel Xeon 2.8 GHz processor with AVX-512 and AES instruction sets
Average multi-core performance suitable for web serving and database tasks
No bundled local storage or GPU acceleration
Appropriate for software compilation, web serving, and general-purpose applications
